intake.user_request: Code intelligence leverage audit and wiring plan for CODEREF-CORE. Tree-sitter now default (WO-SCANNER-AST-COVERAGE-001 closed). Priority gaps: foundation-docs coverage (2382/2791 elements missing headers, RAG at 15%), deferred features (export edges, what-imports/what-depends-on divergence, rag-search --constraint, graph visualization fields, ai-prompt-generator smart truncation), and workflow wiring.

classification.plan_type_rationale: Multiple independent improvement areas (source-headers coverage, tree-sitter field verification, 5 deferred features) each requiring its own verification gate. No single risky system boundary change — sequential delivery reduces blast radius and allows each phase to ship independently.

in_scope: Run --source-headers on CODEREF-CORE and commit (2382/2791 elements missing)
Verify tree-sitter enrichment fields (returnType, decorators, docstring, complexity) for TS/JS/Go/Rust/Java/C++
Fix export edges never emitted in graph-builder.ts
Fix what-imports vs what-depends-on divergence (both call getDependencies())
Populate graph visualization fields (nodePositions, nodeColors, edgeWeights, layoutHints)
Add ai-prompt-generator.ts smart truncation that preserves structure
out_of_scope: close-workorder collision check (workflow wiring — separate concern)

constraints: Each phase must be independently shippable with passing tests
RAG coverage improvement is the highest-leverage item — source-headers runs first
Tree-sitter field verification is read-only audit before any fix work
No breaking changes to public ElementData or GraphData interfaces
